title: TMDB Keyword Lexicon — NRC Emotion + VAD + SCL Valence Scores
subtitle: 67,916 TMDB keywords with NRC + SCL phrase-level valence
description:
One row per unique keyword phrase from the TMDB movies dataset (~1.24M theatrical releases), joined to NRC affective lexicons and SCL sentiment composition lexicons by Saif M. Mohammad (NRC Canada).

## What's new in v4

Added composition analysis columns (propagated from the SCL joined lexicon for `in_scl=True` keywords):

- **`composition_shift`** — `valence − computed_valence`: how much phrase meaning deviates from naive token averaging
- **`abs_composition_shift`** — magnitude of shift, sign-agnostic
- **`shift_zscore`** — statistical significance: `|z| ≥ 2` = significant outlier from the shift population
- **`shift_percentile`** — percentile rank of `|shift|` (0–100); top 5% = strong shift
- **`shift_strong`** / **`shift_very_strong`** — absolute threshold flags (`|shift| ≥ 0.30` / `≥ 0.50`)
- **`composition_type`** — linguistic mechanism: `negation` / `degree_modifier` / `modal` / `opposing_polarity` / `idiomatic` / `direct`

These columns are null for `computed` and unmatched keywords (no empirical phrase score to compare against).

**Top TMDB keywords by composition shift:**
- `no love` → −1.372 (negation)
- `sex toy` → −1.267 (idiomatic)
- `car accident` → −0.995 (idiomatic)

## What's new in v3

Added SCL coverage and NMA modifier flags:

- **`in_scl`** — True if the keyword phrase has an exact match in the SCL joined lexicon (OPP, NMA, or VAD)
- **`scl_token_coverage`** — fraction of keyword tokens found in SCL unigrams (0.0–1.0)
- **`valence_scale`** — `bipolar` when valence is not null; confirms all sources use Best-Worst Scaling [-1, +1]
- **`valence_source`** — now uses `computed` (instead of `token_mean`) for token-mean fallback
- **`negators_contained`** — semicolon-separated negator tokens found in keyword (e.g. `not`, `never`, `no`)
- **`modals_contained`** — semicolon-separated modal tokens (e.g. `can`, `should`, `might`)
- **`adverbs_contained`** — semicolon-separated intensifier/diminisher adverbs (e.g. `very`, `barely`)
- **`has_nma`** — True if any negator, modal, or adverb is present

## What's new in v2

Added phrase-level valence columns from the Sentiment Composition Lexicons (SCL):

- **SCL-OPP** — opposing-polarity phrases (e.g. `happy accident`, `bad luck`)
- **SCL-NMA** — negator/modal/adverb phrases (e.g. `not bad`, `barely good`)

These are joined using a valence hierarchy that selects the most specific score available:

```
valence_source precedence: opp > nma > vad > computed
```

Composition shift is only defined for `in_scl=True` keywords (direct SCL phrase match).

```
```

This fixes the known limitation of token-level averaging for phrases with mixed polarity.

## Columns (46 total)

| Column | Description |
|---|---|
| `keyword` | Raw TMDB keyword phrase |
| `n_movies` | Number of TMDB movies using this keyword |
| `emolex_found` | Found in NRC Emotion Lexicon |
| `intensity_found` | Found in NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon |
| `vad_found` | Found in NRC VAD v2.1 |
| `emolex_anger` … `emolex_negative` | Binary union across tokens (8 emotions + pos/neg) |
| `intensity_anger` … `intensity_disgust` | Mean intensity across matched tokens (0–1) |
| `vad_valence` | Mean keyword valence, bipolar −1 to +1 (NRC VAD v2.1, token-mean) |
| `vad_arousal` | Mean keyword arousal, bipolar −1 to +1 |
| `vad_dominance` | Mean keyword dominance, bipolar −1 to +1 |
| `in_scl` | True if keyword is an exact phrase in the SCL joined lexicon |
| `scl_token_coverage` | Fraction of keyword tokens (0.0–1.0) found as SCL unigrams |
| `valence` | **Best available valence** — bipolar [-1, +1] — following OPP > NMA > VAD > computed hierarchy |
| `valence_scale` | `bipolar` when valence is not null. All sources use Best-Worst Scaling [-1, +1]. |
| `valence_source` | Which source supplied the valence: `opp` / `nma` / `vad` (direct SCL) or `computed` (token mean) |
| `valence_count` | Number of SCL lexicons with a score (0–3). Higher = more confident. |
| `valence_variance` | Variance across lexicon scores (null if < 2 sources — use as confidence signal) |
| `matched_tokens` | Tokens with a valence score (relevant for `computed` source) |
| `total_tokens` | Total tokens in keyword |
| `negators_contained` | Semicolon-separated negator tokens in keyword (e.g. `not;never`) |
| `modals_contained` | Semicolon-separated modal tokens in keyword (e.g. `can;should`) |
| `adverbs_contained` | Semicolon-separated intensifier/diminisher adverbs (e.g. `very;barely`) |
| `has_nma` | True if any negator, modal, or adverb is present in the keyword |

## Lexicon rules

- **EmoLex (binary):** union across tokens — if any token has `fear=1`, the keyword gets `emolex_fear=1`
- **Intensity:** mean across matched tokens (token-level lemmatization via NLTK WordNet)
- **VAD v2.1:** full-phrase MWE lookup first, then token mean
- **SCL-OPP / SCL-NMA:** exact phrase lookup; OPP takes precedence over NMA when both match
- **`computed` fallback:** mean of hierarchy-selected unigram `valence` scores (not raw VAD) — OPP/NMA scores propagate through token fallback

## Coverage

| Lexicon | Keywords matched | % |
|---|---|---|
| NRC Emotion Lexicon (EmoLex) | 37,678 | 55.5% |
| NRC Emotion Intensity | 21,405 | 31.5% |
| NRC VAD v2.1 | 44,606 | 65.7% |
| `valence` (any source) | 38,511 | 56.7% |
| SCL exact match (`in_scl`) | 11,649 | 17.2% |
| Keywords with NMA context (`has_nma`) | 190 | 0.3% |

## Data provenance

Keywords extracted from: [asaniczka/tmdb-movies-dataset-2023-930k-movies](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/asaniczka/tmdb-movies-dataset-2023-930k-movies) (sourced from TMDb API). Adult-labeled movies excluded.

NRC Lexicons: [saifmohammad.com/WebPages/lexicons.html](https://saifmohammad.com/WebPages/lexicons.html)

SCL Lexicons: [saifmohammad.com/WebPages/SCL.html](https://www.saifmohammad.com/WebPages/SCL.html)

## Related datasets

- [TMDB Movie VAD + Emotion Scores](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/bdelanghe/tmdb-movie-vad-emotion-scores) — movie-level aggregation of these keyword scores
- [SCL Joined Lexicon](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/bdelanghe/scl-joined-lexicon) — the underlying NRC VAD + OPP + NMA join used to produce these scores

**Source pipeline:** [github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle](https://github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle)

resources.description: 67,916 unique TMDB keyword phrases with NRC EmoLex, Intensity, VAD v2.1, and SCL phrase-level valence scores plus NMA modifier flags. One row per keyword.

fields.name: keyword
fields.description: Raw TMDB keyword phrase (e.g. 'child abuse', 'space marine')

fields.name: n_movies
fields.description: Number of TMDB movies tagged with this keyword

fields.name: emolex_found
fields.description: True if any token matched NRC Emotion Lexicon

fields.name: intensity_found
fields.description: True if any token matched NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon

fields.name: vad_found
fields.description: True if phrase or any token matched NRC VAD v2.1

fields.name: emolex_anger
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with anger (binary union)

fields.name: emolex_anticipation
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with anticipation

fields.name: emolex_disgust
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with disgust

fields.name: emolex_fear
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with fear

fields.name: emolex_joy
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with joy

fields.name: emolex_sadness
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with sadness

fields.name: emolex_surprise
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with surprise

fields.name: emolex_trust
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token associates with trust

fields.name: emolex_positive
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token is positive-sentiment

fields.name: emolex_negative
fields.description: NRC EmoLex: 1 if any token is negative-sentiment

fields.name: intensity_anger
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean anger intensity across matched tokens (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_anticipation
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean anticipation intensity (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_disgust
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean disgust intensity (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_fear
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean fear intensity (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_joy
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean joy intensity (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_sadness
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean sadness intensity (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_surprise
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean surprise intensity (0–1)

fields.name: intensity_trust
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean trust intensity (0–1)

fields.name: vad_valence
fields.description: NRC VAD v2.1: mean keyword valence, bipolar −1 (negative) to +1 (positive). MWE-first lookup, then token mean.

fields.name: vad_arousal
fields.description: NRC VAD v2.1: mean keyword arousal, bipolar −1 (calm) to +1 (excited)

fields.name: vad_dominance
fields.description: NRC VAD v2.1: mean keyword dominance, bipolar −1 (submissive) to +1 (dominant)

fields.name: in_scl
fields.description: True if keyword phrase has an exact match in the SCL joined lexicon (any of VAD, OPP, or NMA)

fields.name: scl_token_coverage
fields.description: Fraction of keyword tokens (0.0–1.0) found as unigrams in the SCL joined lexicon

fields.name: valence
fields.description: Best available bipolar valence [−1, +1] following OPP > NMA > VAD > computed hierarchy. Scale: Best-Worst Scaling.

fields.name: valence_scale
fields.description: Always `bipolar` when valence is not null. Confirms scale is Best-Worst Scaling [-1, +1] across all sources (VAD, OPP, NMA, computed).

fields.name: valence_source
fields.description: Source of valence: opp (SCL-OPP), nma (SCL-NMA), vad (NRC VAD exact match), computed (token-mean fallback using hierarchy-selected unigram scores)

fields.name: valence_count
fields.description: Number of SCL lexicons (0–3) with a valence score for this term. Higher = more confident.

fields.name: valence_variance
fields.description: Variance across lexicon valence scores. Non-null only when valence_count >= 2. High variance = lexicons disagree.

fields.name: matched_tokens
fields.description: Number of tokens with a valence score (relevant for computed source)

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: Total word count of keyword phrase

fields.name: negators_contained
fields.description: Semicolon-separated negator tokens found in keyword using word-boundary match (e.g. 'not', 'never', 'no', 'cannot'). Negators invert or reduce polarity.

fields.name: modals_contained
fields.description: Semicolon-separated modal tokens found in keyword (e.g. 'can', 'could', 'should', 'might', 'must', 'would'). Modals express conditionality.

fields.name: adverbs_contained
fields.description: Semicolon-separated intensifier/diminisher adverbs found in keyword (e.g. 'very', 'quite', 'barely', 'extremely'). Adverbs scale the head word's polarity.

fields.name: has_nma
fields.description: True if any negator, modal, or adverb is present in the keyword. Signals potential composition effects where phrase valence differs from token averages.

fields.name: composition_shift
fields.description: valence − computed_valence. Only populated for in_scl=True keywords. Measures how much phrase valence deviates from naive token averaging.

fields.name: abs_composition_shift
fields.description: |composition_shift|. Magnitude of semantic nonlinearity. Null for computed and unmatched keywords.

fields.name: shift_zscore
fields.description: Z-score of composition_shift from the SCL joined lexicon population. |z| ≥ 2 = statistically significant shift. Null for computed/unmatched.

fields.name: shift_percentile
fields.description: Percentile rank (0–100) of abs_composition_shift in the SCL lexicon. Top 5% = strong; top 1% = extreme. Null for computed/unmatched.

fields.name: shift_strong
fields.description: True if abs_composition_shift ≥ 0.30 (meaningful composition effect). Null for computed/unmatched.

fields.name: shift_very_strong
fields.description: True if abs_composition_shift ≥ 0.50 (very strong composition effect). Null for computed/unmatched.

fields.name: composition_type
fields.description: Linguistic mechanism driving shift. For in_scl=True: negation / degree_modifier / modal / opposing_polarity / idiomatic / nma_unclassified / direct. Null for computed/unmatched.
